articles.title: Bright lights and hot orbs: UFO files shed light on sightings but leave interpretation to the public
articles.summary: On Friday the Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files, including videos, images, State Department cables, FBI documents, and NASA transcripts, as President Trump urged the public to draw their own conclusions. The documents describe a range of sightings, such as a 1994 Tajikistan cable of a brightly lit object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan, a 2023 Aegean Sea report of a UAP turning at 80 mph, a 2023 helicopter encounter with a “super‑hot” orb, and a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief in which Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that could have been a laser.

en.title: Bright lights and hot orbs: UFO files shed light on sightings but leave interpretation to the public
en.summary: On Friday the Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files, including videos, images, State Department cables, FBI documents, and NASA transcripts, as President Trump urged the public to draw their own conclusions. The documents describe a range of sightings, such as a 1994 Tajikistan cable of a brightly lit object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan, a 2023 Aegean Sea report of a UAP turning at 80 mph, a 2023 helicopter encounter with a “super‑hot” orb, and a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief in which Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that could have been a laser.

de.title: Helle Lichter und heiße Kugeln: UFO-Akten geben Aufschluss über Sichtungen, überlassen die Interpretation jedoch der Öffentlichkeit
de.summary: Am Freitag veröffentlichte das Pentagon einen neuen Stapel freigegebener UFO-Dateien, darunter Videos, Bilder, Depeschen des Außenministeriums, FBI-Dokumente und NASA-Transkripte, als Präsident Trump die Öffentlichkeit aufforderte, ihre eigenen Schlussfolgerungen zu ziehen. Die Dokumente beschreiben eine Reihe von Sichtungen, wie zum Beispiel ein Tadschikistan-Kabel eines hell erleuchteten Objekts aus dem Jahr 1994, das sich um 90 Grad über Kasachstan dreht, ein Bericht aus dem Jahr 2023 über eine UAP aus dem Ägäischen Meer, der sich mit 80 Meilen pro Stunde dreht, eine Hubschrauberbegegnung mit einer „superheißen“ Kugel aus dem Jahr 2023 und eine Nachbesprechung von Apollo 11 aus dem Jahr 1969, in der Buzz Aldrin ein helles Licht bemerkte, bei dem es sich um einen Laser gehandelt haben könnte.

articles.title: Sleeping Dog: How New UFO Documentary Could Bring Us Closer To Real-Life Disclosure Day
articles.summary: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.

en.title: Sleeping Dog: How New UFO Documentary Could Bring Us Closer To Real-Life Disclosure Day
en.summary: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.

de.title: Sleeping Dog: Wie uns eine neue UFO-Dokumentation dem Tag der Enthüllung im wirklichen Leben näher bringen könnte
de.summary: Der Journalist und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell bespricht seinen neuen Dokumentarfilm mit NBC News-Korrespondent Gadi Schwartz. Der Dokumentarfilm trägt den Titel „Sleeping Dog“. Es untersucht Corbells Umgang mit Regierungsgeheimnissen. Es geht auch um Whistleblower und seine Suche nach der Wahrheit über UFOs.

articles.title: Ukrainian Defence Ministry Advisor Drops Bizarre UFOs Encountered By Military Amid Pentagon's Disclosure [WATCH]
articles.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior advisor to Ukraine's Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have a formal program for tracking and studying unidentified flying objects that has been active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program operates under an official directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and is considered a national security necessity because unidentified aerial activity could indicate new Russian weapons technology.

en.title: Ukrainian Defence Ministry Advisor Drops Bizarre UFOs Encountered By Military Amid Pentagon's Disclosure [WATCH]
en.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior advisor to Ukraine's Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have a formal program for tracking and studying unidentified flying objects that has been active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program operates under an official directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and is considered a national security necessity because unidentified aerial activity could indicate new Russian weapons technology.

de.title: Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministeriums lässt bizarre UFOs fallen, auf die das Militär gestoßen ist, nachdem das Pentagon dies offengelegt hat [ANSCHAUEN]
de.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, ein leitender Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministers, gab bekannt, dass die ukrainischen Streitkräfte über ein formelles Programm zur Verfolgung und Untersuchung nicht identifizierter Flugobjekte verfügen, das seit den frühen Tagen des Krieges mit Russland aktiv ist. Er sagte, das Programm liege auf einer vom Oberbefehlshaber genehmigten offiziellen Weisung und werde als nationale Sicherheitsnotwendigkeit angesehen, da nicht identifizierte Luftaktivitäten auf neue russische Waffentechnologie hinweisen könnten.

articles.title: Longmont’s UFO Files: National Database Details Decades Of Strange Sightings
articles.summary: A national UFO database lists more than 100 reports from Longmont residents and travelers describing metallic spheres, triangular and chevron‑shaped craft, orange lights, green fireballs, and other unusual objects over the city. ” Earlier reports date back to 1998, when a family saw a large triangular craft near the airport, and 1999, when a saucer‑shaped object with flashing lights was observed from a window.

en.summary: A national UFO database lists more than 100 reports from Longmont residents and travelers describing metallic spheres, triangular and chevron‑shaped craft, orange lights, green fireballs, and other unusual objects over the city. ” Earlier reports date back to 1998, when a family saw a large triangular craft near the airport, and 1999, when a saucer‑shaped object with flashing lights was observed from a window.

de.title: Longmonts UFO-Dateien: Nationale Datenbank beschreibt jahrzehntelange seltsame Sichtungen
de.summary: Eine nationale UFO-Datenbank listet mehr als 100 Berichte von Einwohnern und Reisenden von Longmont auf, in denen Metallkugeln, dreieckige und winkelförmige Fahrzeuge, orangefarbene Lichter, grüne Feuerbälle und andere ungewöhnliche Objekte über der Stadt beschrieben werden. „Frühere Berichte stammen aus dem Jahr 1998, als eine Familie ein großes dreieckiges Flugzeug in der Nähe des Flughafens sah, und aus dem Jahr 1999, als aus einem Fenster ein untertassenförmiges Objekt mit blinkenden Lichtern beobachtet wurde.

articles.title: Pentagon’s ‘Immaculate Constellation’ Allegations Ignite New UFO Disclosure Firestorm In Congress
articles.summary: Whistleblower claims introduced in a November 2024 House Oversight hearing allege that a hidden, AI‑driven program called “Immaculate Constellation” collects and scrubs UAP data from global surveillance feeds, operating as an unacknowledged Special Access Program that may bypass congressional oversight. The allegations, presented by journalist Michael Shellenberger, say the program centralizes classified military footage and sensor data on UAP encounters, including reports of aircraft boxed by spheres and a glowing object near a Navy carrier group.

en.title: Pentagon’s ‘Immaculate Constellation’ Allegations Ignite New UFO Disclosure Firestorm In Congress
en.summary: Whistleblower claims introduced in a November 2024 House Oversight hearing allege that a hidden, AI‑driven program called “Immaculate Constellation” collects and scrubs UAP data from global surveillance feeds, operating as an unacknowledged Special Access Program that may bypass congressional oversight. The allegations, presented by journalist Michael Shellenberger, say the program centralizes classified military footage and sensor data on UAP encounters, including reports of aircraft boxed by spheres and a glowing object near a Navy carrier group.

de.title: Die Anschuldigungen des Pentagons wegen der „Unbefleckten Konstellation“ entfachen im Kongress einen neuen Feuersturm zur Enthüllung von UFOs
de.summary: Whistleblower-Behauptungen, die in einer Anhörung zum House Oversight im November 2024 vorgebracht wurden, behaupten, dass ein verstecktes, KI-gesteuertes Programm namens „Immaculate Constellation“ UAP-Daten aus globalen Überwachungs-Feeds sammelt und bereinigt und als nicht anerkanntes Sonderzugriffsprogramm fungiert, das die Aufsicht des Kongresses umgehen könnte. Die vom Journalisten Michael Shellenberger vorgetragenen Behauptungen besagen, dass das Programm vertrauliches militärisches Filmmaterial und Sensordaten zu UAP-Begegnungen zentralisiert, darunter Berichte über von Kugeln umgebene Flugzeuge und ein leuchtendes Objekt in der Nähe einer Trägergruppe der Marine.

articles.title: Illusionist Uri Geller Reacts To US Congressman's Warning Over Next Set Of UFO Files: 'Holy Crap Is Coming'
articles.summary: Israeli‑British illusionist Uri Geller, 79, reacted to US Congressman Tim Burchett’s warning that the next batch of declassified UFO files will dwarf those already released, saying he believes every word of the warning. Burchett, a member of the UAP Caucus, told Joe Rogan that the first set of 162 documents was only a “drop in the bucket” and that “Holy Crap is coming” with future releases.

Burchett, a member of the UAP Caucus, told Joe Rogan that the first set of 162 documents was only a “drop in the bucket” and that “Holy Crap is coming” with future releases.

de.title: Der Illusionist Uri Geller reagiert auf die Warnung des US-Kongressabgeordneten vor der nächsten Reihe von UFO-Dateien: „Heiliger Mist kommt“
de.summary: Der israelisch-britische Illusionist Uri Geller, 79, reagierte auf die Warnung des US-Kongressabgeordneten Tim Burchett, dass die nächste Ladung freigegebener UFO-Dateien die bereits freigegebenen in den Schatten stellen werde, und sagte, er glaube jedes Wort der Warnung. Burchett, ein Mitglied des UAP Caucus, sagte Joe Rogan, dass der erste Satz von 162 Dokumenten nur ein „Tropfen auf den heißen Stein“ sei und dass mit zukünftigen Veröffentlichungen „heiliger Mist kommt“.

articles.title: A small Oregon town puts on a big UFO festival. Why it's worth a visit
articles.summary: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17, 2026, and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. The festival celebrates the 1950 UFO sighting by Paul and Evelyn Trent, whose photographs of a metallic object sparked national debate and are considered among the most famous UFO images.

qualityBreakdown.label: Basis
qualityBreakdown.text: UAP/UFO-Bezug erkannt und Unterhaltung/Gaming herausgefiltert.

qualityBreakdown.label: Starker Titel
qualityBreakdown.text: Der Titel enthält einen klaren UAP/UFO-Bezug.

qualityBreakdown.label: Kernaussage
qualityBreakdown.text: Ein besonders relevantes UAP-Thema wird erkannt.

articles.qualityExplanation: Die Punkte zeigen UAP-Bezug, starke Begriffe, offizielle Stellen, Quellenvertrauen, mehrere Quellen und bei schwachen Einzelquellen einen vorsichtigen Abzug.

en.title: A small Oregon town puts on a big UFO festival. Why it's worth a visit
en.summary: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17, 2026, and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. The festival celebrates the 1950 UFO sighting by Paul and Evelyn Trent, whose photographs of a metallic object sparked national debate and are considered among the most famous UFO images.

de.title: Eine kleine Stadt in Oregon veranstaltet ein großes UFO-Festival. Warum es einen Besuch wert ist
de.summary: Das 26. jährliche McMenamins UFO-Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, findet vom 14. bis 17. Mai 2026 statt und wird vom McMenamins Hotel Oregon ausgerichtet. Damit ist es nach Roswell das zweitgrößte UFO-Festival in den Vereinigten Staaten. Das Festival feiert die UFO-Sichtung von Paul und Evelyn Trent im Jahr 1950, deren Fotografien eines metallischen Objekts eine landesweite Debatte auslösten und zu den berühmtesten UFO-Bildern zählen.

summaries.file-release-bright-lights-and-hot-orbs-: On Friday the Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files, including videos, images, State Department cables, FBI documents, and NASA transcripts, as President Trump urged the public to draw their own conclusions. The documents describe a range of sightings, such as a 1994 Tajikistan cable of a brightly lit object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan, a 2023 Aegean Sea report of a UAP turning at 80 mph, a 2023 helicopter encounter with a “super‑hot” orb, and a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief in which Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that could have been a laser. Other files include a 2023 drone‑pilot interview of a linear object, a 1972 Apollo 17 photo of three dots in a triangular formation, and more than 20 military videos from locations such as Syria, Japan, and North America showing fast‑moving specks and larger objects. The Pentagon notes that the cases are unresolved and that there is no consensus on the nature of the anomalies, with a preliminary analysis suggesting some could be physical objects but no evidence of alien technology. Experts and congressional members have urged caution and further transparency, with some calling for a thorough review of classified UAP records, while the 2024 Pentagon report confirmed no proof of recovered alien technology.
summaries.film-sleeping-dog-how-new-ufo-documentar: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.
summaries.program-ukrainian-defence-ministry-advis: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior advisor to Ukraine's Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have a formal program for tracking and studying unidentified flying objects that has been active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program operates under an official directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and is considered a national security necessity because unidentified aerial activity could indicate new Russian weapons technology. Beskrestnov posted 2023 frontline reconnaissance footage of an aerial anomaly and later received a message from officials of a Ukrainian state institution that has handled such investigations since the Soviet era. He urged military personnel and civilians who capture unidentified objects on camera to submit their footage to uap@gur.gov.ua, noting that firsthand visual evidence could have significant intelligence value. His remarks came as the U.S. Pentagon released a declassified set of 162 UFO files on May 8, 2026, following a directive from President Trump.
summaries.ufo-files-in-pursuit-of-pies-in-the-sky: On Friday the Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files, including videos, images, State Department cables, FBI documents, and NASA transcripts, as President Trump urged the public to draw their own conclusions. The documents describe a range of sightings, such as a 1994 Tajikistan cable of a brightly lit object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan, a 2023 Aegean Sea report of a UAP turning at 80 mph, a 2023 helicopter encounter with a “super‑hot” orb, and a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief in which Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that could have been a laser. Other files include a 2023 drone‑pilot interview of a linear object, a 1972 Apollo 17 photo of three dots in a triangular formation, and more than 20 military videos from locations such as Syria, Japan, and North America showing fast‑moving specks and larger objects. The Pentagon notes that the cases are unresolved and that there is no consensus on the nature of the anomalies, with a preliminary analysis suggesting some could be physical objects but no evidence of alien technology. Experts and congressional members have urged caution and further transparency, with some calling for a thorough review of classified UAP records, while the 2024 Pentagon report confirmed no proof of recovered alien technology.
summaries.ufo-files-reveal-the-same-old-material: On Friday the Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files, including videos, images, State Department cables, FBI documents, and NASA transcripts, as President Trump urged the public to draw their own conclusions. The documents describe a range of sightings, such as a 1994 Tajikistan cable of a brightly lit object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan, a 2023 Aegean Sea report of a UAP turning at 80 mph, a 2023 helicopter encounter with a “super‑hot” orb, and a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief in which Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that could have been a laser. Other files include a 2023 drone‑pilot interview of a linear object, a 1972 Apollo 17 photo of three dots in a triangular formation, and more than 20 military videos from locations such as Syria, Japan, and North America showing fast‑moving specks and larger objects. The Pentagon notes that the cases are unresolved and that there is no consensus on the nature of the anomalies, with a preliminary analysis suggesting some could be physical objects but no evidence of alien technology. Experts and congressional members have urged caution and further transparency, with some calling for a thorough review of classified UAP records, while the 2024 Pentagon report confirmed no proof of recovered alien technology.
summaries.sighting-longmont-s-ufo-files-national-d: A national UFO database lists more than 100 reports from Longmont residents and travelers describing metallic spheres, triangular and chevron‑shaped craft, orange lights, green fireballs, and other unusual objects over the city. Recent entries include a March 25 2025 sighting of a hovering metallic sphere that lasted about 20 minutes, an October 14 2025 dark triangular craft with white lights and a muted hum, and a January 27 2026 silver chevron‑shaped object that was classified by NUFORC as “Aircraft — Probable.” Earlier reports date back to 1998, when a family saw a large triangular craft near the airport, and 1999, when a saucer‑shaped object with flashing lights was observed from a window. The database notes that many sightings could be explained by aircraft, satellites, drones, weather balloons, or optical effects, and that NUFORC includes cautionary notes or possible explanations for each case. The reports provide a public record of what witnesses said they saw, but they do not constitute official confirmation of extraterrestrial activity, and no definitive conclusion has been reached.
summaries.pentagon-s-immaculate-constellation-alle: Whistleblower claims introduced in a November 2024 House Oversight hearing allege that a hidden, AI‑driven program called “Immaculate Constellation” collects and scrubs UAP data from global surveillance feeds, operating as an unacknowledged Special Access Program that may bypass congressional oversight. The allegations, presented by journalist Michael Shellenberger, say the program centralizes classified military footage and sensor data on UAP encounters, including reports of aircraft boxed by spheres and a glowing object near a Navy carrier group. Pentagon spokesperson Sue Gough and former AARO director Sean Kirkpatrick have denied the existence of such a program, stating the Department of Defense has no record of an SAP by that name. No publicly released evidence has verified the claims, and the allegations remain unconfirmed, leaving the legal and constitutional implications of a potential covert program unresolved. Congress has responded by demanding greater transparency on UAP investigations, defense spending, and compartmentalized aerospace programs, while the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office has stated it has found no evidence of extraterrestrial technology.
summaries.translucent-beings-pastors-at-ufo-disclo: The U.S. Department of War released 162 UFO‑related files in the first tranche of the UFO Disclosure 2026, including classified NASA documents on UAP anomalies observed by Apollo astronauts such as Buzz Aldrin’s 1969 lunar landing sightings. U.S. Congressman Tim Burchett said the next set of UFO files would “blow laymen’s minds” and described it as “holy crap is coming.” Pastors Joseph Zupetz and Tony Merkel were briefed on May 8 2026 and reported that church leaders were shown images of translucent extraterrestrial entities with long limbs in wooded settings, which they said could fulfill a dark biblical prophecy. The pastors claimed that intelligence officials compared the disclosures to passages in Revelation and the Epistles of Paul about a “Great Deception” in the end times, and that God would send strong delusion to those who believe the lie. Other pastors, including Greg Locke, tweeted that the UFO discussion is a cover‑up for a demonic release and that a great deception is imminent as the age ends. The article notes that the interpretation of the evidence remains uncertain and that evangelical theories vary.
summaries.illusionist-uri-geller-reacts-to-us-cong: Israeli‑British illusionist Uri Geller, 79, reacted to US Congressman Tim Burchett’s warning that the next batch of declassified UFO files will dwarf those already released, saying he believes every word of the warning. Burchett, a member of the UAP Caucus, told Joe Rogan that the first set of 162 documents was only a “drop in the bucket” and that “Holy Crap is coming” with future releases. He cited reports of pilots being pulled from duty for psychological evaluation after UAP encounters, and alleged sightings of underwater craft moving over 200 mph and as large as a football field. Geller tweeted that in the 1970s he was shown fragments of metal from a crashed UFO by rocket scientist Wernher von Braun, and that he later saw non‑human bodies stored at NASA’s Goddard Space Center. He claims the already released files, which include Apollo and Gemini mission reports of strange lights and objects, validate his long‑standing assertions and hopes the forthcoming documents will expose NASA’s full involvement.
summaries.aliens-nephilim-boebert-weighs-in-on-uap: Last week the U.S. Department of War released previously classified documents on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena, with the White House, ODNI, DOE, AARO, NASA, FBI and other intelligence agencies involved, and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced the release as a step toward unprecedented transparency. Rep. Lauren Boebert discussed UAPs on the Breakthrough Hour podcast, citing biblical references to fallen angels and Nephilim and questioning a rumored Department of Defense project that might involve human genetics and hybrids, a line of inquiry she also raised during a 2024 House Oversight hearing. Colorado has a history of UAP activity, including 1970s cattle mutilations, 2020 drone sightings over fields near Senator Cory Gardner’s home, and a 2019 report of 30–50 drones with a larger mothership, but investigations have ruled out military involvement and left the origin and purpose of the drones unknown. The 2024 release included videos of orb‑like objects similar to the 2017 “Tic‑Tacs,” an eight‑pointed chandelier‑shaped UAP that may be a flare, and NASA Apollo 12 and Apollo 17 images and transcripts that show unexplained dots and objects near spacecraft, yet no definitive evidence of extraterrestrial life emerged. Public speculation remains high, with figures such as
summaries.a-small-oregon-town-puts-on-a-big-ufo-fe: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17, 2026, and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. The festival celebrates the 1950 UFO sighting by Paul and Evelyn Trent, whose photographs of a metallic object sparked national debate and are considered among the most famous UFO images. Organizers describe the event as a gathering where skeptics and believers meet, offering speakers, live entertainment, a parade, costume contests, a trivia session, and a costume ball. Ticketed speaker presentations are available through ufofest.com, while most other activities are free, and the schedule includes a “UAP pass pickup party,” a podcast recording, and a panel with an undisclosed whistleblower interviewed by Jeremy Corbell. The festival coincides with the release of previously unseen federal UFO files, adding new context to the town’s long‑standing UFO lore.

en.title: Bright lights and hot orbs: UFO files shed light on sightings but leave interpretation to the public
en.summary: On Friday the Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files, including videos, images, State Department cables, FBI documents, and NASA transcripts, as President Trump urged the public to draw their own conclusions. The documents describe a range of sightings, such as a 1994 Tajikistan cable of a brightly lit object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan, a 2023 Aegean Sea report of a UAP turning at 80 mph, a 2023 helicopter encounter with a “super‑hot” orb, and a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief in which Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that could have been a laser.

de.title: Helle Lichter und heiße Kugeln: UFO-Akten geben Aufschluss über Sichtungen, überlassen die Interpretation jedoch der Öffentlichkeit
de.summary: Am Freitag veröffentlichte das Pentagon einen neuen Stapel freigegebener UFO-Dateien, darunter Videos, Bilder, Depeschen des Außenministeriums, FBI-Dokumente und NASA-Transkripte, als Präsident Trump die Öffentlichkeit aufforderte, ihre eigenen Schlussfolgerungen zu ziehen. Die Dokumente beschreiben eine Reihe von Sichtungen, wie zum Beispiel ein Tadschikistan-Kabel eines hell erleuchteten Objekts aus dem Jahr 1994, das sich um 90 Grad über Kasachstan dreht, ein Bericht aus dem Jahr 2023 über eine UAP aus dem Ägäischen Meer, der sich mit 80 Meilen pro Stunde dreht, eine Hubschrauberbegegnung mit einer „superheißen“ Kugel aus dem Jahr 2023 und eine Nachbesprechung von Apollo 11 aus dem Jahr 1969, in der Buzz Aldrin ein helles Licht bemerkte, bei dem es sich um einen Laser gehandelt haben könnte.

en.title: Sleeping Dog: How New UFO Documentary Could Bring Us Closer To Real-Life Disclosure Day
en.summary: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.

de.title: Sleeping Dog: Wie uns eine neue UFO-Dokumentation dem Tag der Enthüllung im wirklichen Leben näher bringen könnte
de.summary: Der Journalist und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell bespricht seinen neuen Dokumentarfilm mit NBC News-Korrespondent Gadi Schwartz. Der Dokumentarfilm trägt den Titel „Sleeping Dog“. Es untersucht Corbells Umgang mit Regierungsgeheimnissen. Es geht auch um Whistleblower und seine Suche nach der Wahrheit über UFOs.

en.title: Ukrainian Defence Ministry Advisor Drops Bizarre UFOs Encountered By Military Amid Pentagon's Disclosure [WATCH]
en.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior advisor to Ukraine's Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have a formal program for tracking and studying unidentified flying objects that has been active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program operates under an official directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and is considered a national security necessity because unidentified aerial activity could indicate new Russian weapons technology.

de.title: Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministeriums lässt bizarre UFOs fallen, auf die das Militär gestoßen ist, nachdem das Pentagon dies offengelegt hat [ANSCHAUEN]
de.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, ein leitender Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministers, gab bekannt, dass die ukrainischen Streitkräfte über ein formelles Programm zur Verfolgung und Untersuchung nicht identifizierter Flugobjekte verfügen, das seit den frühen Tagen des Krieges mit Russland aktiv ist. Er sagte, das Programm liege auf einer vom Oberbefehlshaber genehmigten offiziellen Weisung und werde als nationale Sicherheitsnotwendigkeit angesehen, da nicht identifizierte Luftaktivitäten auf neue russische Waffentechnologie hinweisen könnten.

de.title: Longmonts UFO-Dateien: Nationale Datenbank beschreibt jahrzehntelange seltsame Sichtungen
de.summary: Eine nationale UFO-Datenbank listet mehr als 100 Berichte von Einwohnern und Reisenden von Longmont auf, in denen Metallkugeln, dreieckige und winkelförmige Fahrzeuge, orangefarbene Lichter, grüne Feuerbälle und andere ungewöhnliche Objekte über der Stadt beschrieben werden. „Frühere Berichte stammen aus dem Jahr 1998, als eine Familie ein großes dreieckiges Flugzeug in der Nähe des Flughafens sah, und aus dem Jahr 1999, als aus einem Fenster ein untertassenförmiges Objekt mit blinkenden Lichtern beobachtet wurde.

en.title: Pentagon’s ‘Immaculate Constellation’ Allegations Ignite New UFO Disclosure Firestorm In Congress
en.summary: Whistleblower claims introduced in a November 2024 House Oversight hearing allege that a hidden, AI‑driven program called “Immaculate Constellation” collects and scrubs UAP data from global surveillance feeds, operating as an unacknowledged Special Access Program that may bypass congressional oversight. The allegations, presented by journalist Michael Shellenberger, say the program centralizes classified military footage and sensor data on UAP encounters, including reports of aircraft boxed by spheres and a glowing object near a Navy carrier group.

de.title: Die Anschuldigungen des Pentagons wegen der „Unbefleckten Konstellation“ entfachen im Kongress einen neuen Feuersturm zur Enthüllung von UFOs
de.summary: Whistleblower-Behauptungen, die in einer Anhörung zum House Oversight im November 2024 vorgebracht wurden, behaupten, dass ein verstecktes, KI-gesteuertes Programm namens „Immaculate Constellation“ UAP-Daten aus globalen Überwachungs-Feeds sammelt und bereinigt und als nicht anerkanntes Sonderzugriffsprogramm fungiert, das die Aufsicht des Kongresses umgehen könnte. Die vom Journalisten Michael Shellenberger vorgetragenen Behauptungen besagen, dass das Programm vertrauliches militärisches Filmmaterial und Sensordaten zu UAP-Begegnungen zentralisiert, darunter Berichte über von Kugeln umgebene Flugzeuge und ein leuchtendes Objekt in der Nähe einer Trägergruppe der Marine.
